name: Lamivudine
summary: Approved nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitor being repurposed experimentally to suppress retrotransposon-linked reverse-transcription biology in aging and neurodegeneration contexts.
mechanism_summary: Inhibits reverse transcriptase activity; in aging research, the hypothesized target is endogenous retrotransposon cDNA generation and downstream innate immune activation rather than HIV replication.

open_questions: Whether 3TC reduces retrotransposon activity directly in aging-relevant human tissues
Whether biomarker movement in early Alzheimer's disease replicates in controlled trials
Whether chronic reverse transcriptase inhibition can be used preventively without unacceptable safety or resistance-context tradeoffs
